title: phacelia neomexicana var. euneomexicana
intro: Phacelia neomexicana var. euneomexicana is a variety of the phacelia plant, a flowering perennial native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its distinctive purple flowers and hairy stems. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Phacelia neomexicana var. euneomexicana include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide antioxidant protection. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to support skin health and reduce the risk of infection.
